paragraphs.body: Many people suffer from the fear of being judged by others in today's environment, where social media and public opinion are so important. Our daily decisions, relationships, and even our jobs may be impacted by this worry. Essentially, this fear is a result of worrying about how other people view us, which can cause self-doubt and prevent us from being ourselves. This fear has frequently made me feel as though it is a shadow that hangs over every interaction, controlling my decisions and keeping me from going outside of my comfort zone. Relating to this personally, I do not like to go to the doctor. In the world, many people fear visiting the doctor due to fears based on their age, past, and childhood experiences. The question is, is it because of being afraid of the doctor or because of another psychological reason behind it.

annotations.text: Essentially, this fear is a result of worrying about how other people view us, which can cause self-doubt and prevent us from being ourselves.

paragraphs.body: There are many reasons that one can find a doctor's office scary. In the article, Educate or Placate when Young Child is Afraid of Doctor's Visits, C.S. Mott Children's Hospital explains the research on the fear of doctors in children. They prove their point by developing several different important ideas. First, they included details and research about a poll they conducted on children in doctors' offices. The article that was taken from a study showed different percentages of different occurrences in the doctor's office. It was from getting a shot to just being in the office. Next, they introduced a side of the parents from the children's perspective. It stated how some parents must prepare their children before the doctor's visit so they can relax during the appointment. They included how it has greatly impacted some of the results with anxiety and fear in children. Finally, they provided valuable information about the implications of young children having a big fear in doctor's offices. When children get used to the doctor, their anxiety and fear will eventually go down. It helps the child understand that doctors are not there to hurt them but to help them. It states how common it is to have a fear of doctors, to let people know they are not alone. I relate to this statement about being afraid of doctors.

use present tense when summarizing in MLA format :)

paragraphs.body: The article provided some good points about how fear works and why some kids are affected by it. According to the Children's Hospital, "The biggest culprit behind young children's fear of doctor visits, according to parents, is that the child is scared of getting a shot. While "needle phobia" affects people of all ages, it can be especially tricky for younger children, when vaccinations are frequent" (C.S. Motts Childrens Hospital). In other words, they are trying to show the reasons why children are afraid of doctors and the psychological logical reasoning behind it.. The hospital also did multiple polls to try and help their research to get an answer. So why are kids afraid of the doctors? Is it only based on that reasoning of getting or a shot or is there a social reason behind it? In my life I can agree that this is true of being scared because of lifetime experiences.

paragraphs.body: I have been afraid of doctor's offices all my life. It was difficult for me to stay calm among them whenever I was close to or stepped foot in their presence. When I was younger, I can still recall worrying about when my next well visit would be and attempting to avoid going.

annotations.text: It was difficult for me to stay calm among them whenever I was close to or stepped foot in their

paragraphs.body: It was quite difficult to grow up fearing doctors when they were just attempting to help me. The main issue I had was with needles or shots. I had to get blood drawn frequently while I had Lymes disease, and it was unpleasant to experience this kind of fear. I used to tell myself it was all in my head and never really understood why I used to behave the way that I did. It simply never got around to processing. Needles still give me the creeps. As important as they are, hopefully I will not need them for long. In the future, I hope to move past it.

annotations.text: It was quite difficult to grow up fearing doctors when they were just attempting to help me.

paragraphs.body: I agree that the fear of doctors is true because my experience confirms it. I can only still imagine going and having to try to prepare myself to calm down. It never worked. The one thing about the different doctor's offices that always caught my eye was the different themes in the rooms. I can remember some had animals and some had several types of cartoons for younger kids. They always made the mood and stress ease a little because it was trying to paint a picture with the unusual colors and types of farm animals. Since I am older, I always wonder why I still do not like the doctors. Is it a fear of mine because I had it when I was younger? Is it because of needles? Is the doctor mean? Does a certain test involve blood work? Are all the questions that pop into my mind when thinking about doctors. Looking back at it all since I am in college now, I don't believe it was because of those reasons. I believe it was a social aspect that affected the way I would think in the doctor's situation. The fear of being judged by the doctor ifl would react or run away, as I did as a kid. Not acting like a "fool" was my biggest priority in that situation. Now if I sit back and think about it, it all comes together like a puzzle piece one by one. Even though I have never realized it until recently, it might have been the reason behind the situation of being afraid of doctors. Now trying to meet more people at school has taken this toll on me. Am I scared of them saying something or them being smarter than me? Generally, when I am relaxing somewhere outside or in school or even when I go home for the weekend. I had to think back to all the situations of actually being afraid and it just came together. The thing is, it made me question why I felt this way? What if I didn't care what others thought of me? I guess the answer would be nothing, truly nothing. You are your own person, and you don't have to be perfect in the view of others as long as you are happy with yourself. This motivation can stay with people throughout their life and make experiences way better for them. As I am going to be trying to get a job soon, I will always try to remember the world isn't all out to get you. Just relax and enjoy the people around you. Now that I think about it, I don't believe it was actually going to the doctors, that was the problem, but the social aspects around it.

paragraphs.body: For these reasons, the fear of doctors is a common fear among people. The problem is that isnt always the case among them. Whether it is because of their age, past, or childhood experiences, it is still a problem to be recognized. Coming to realize and having information provided helps another feel not alone during the fright and help them picture something that could possibly calm them down .. Again, doctors are not here to hurt you but only to help you. As I have grown up, it has made me realize you shouldn't be scared to try new things with the fear of judgment from others. Works Cited C.S. Motts Children's Hospital, "Educate or Placate: When Young Children Are Afraid of Doctor Visits." Mott Poll, 2023, https://mottpoll.org/reports/educate-or-placate-when-young- child-afraid-doctors-visits.

annotations.text: I have grown up, it has made me realize you shouldn't be scared to try new things with the fear of judgment from others.
